description: Unify date/timezone handling across the application - all dates in user's selected timezone, trades grouped by entry time

userStories.title: Create Timezone System Documentation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want clear documentation of the timezone system so that future development maintains consistency.
acceptanceCriteria: Add TIMEZONE_SYSTEM.md to docs/ directory explaining the principles
Document which function to use when (decision tree)
Document the 'entry time for grouping' rule
Add examples of correct vs incorrect usage
userStories.notes: Created docs/TIMEZONE_SYSTEM.md with principles, decision tree, function reference, common patterns, and common mistakes.

userStories.title: Create Timezone-Aware Date String Generator
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a utility function that generates date strings for calendar grids using the user's timezone so that calendars display correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Function generates YYYY-MM-DD strings based on user's timezone 'today'
userStories.notes: Added generateDateStringsInTimezone() to timezone.ts. Uses UTC arithmetic to avoid Date object ambiguity. Auto-exported via src/lib/shared/index.ts.

userStories.title: Create Date String Display Utilities
userStories.description: As a developer, I want utilities to get display information from YYYY-MM-DD strings so that calendars can render without Date object ambiguity.
acceptanceCriteria: Add formatDateString(dateStr, format) for display formatting to timezone.ts
Functions work with YYYY-MM-DD strings directly
userStories.notes: Added getDayOfWeekFromDateString(), getMonthFromDateString(), and formatDateString() to timezone.ts. All use UTC date operations to avoid timezone ambiguity. Auto-exported via src/lib/shared/index.ts.

userStories.title: Fix Daily Journal Trade Grouping Bug
userStories.description: As a trader, I want trades in my daily journal grouped by entry time in my timezone so that trades appear on the correct day.
acceptanceCriteria: Trade entered 11 PM EST appears on that EST date, not next day
userStories.notes: Fixed getComplianceStats trade grouping in dailyJournal.ts. Replaced .toISOString().split('T')[0] with getDateStringInTimezone(trade.entryTime, userTimezone). User timezone was already being fetched via getUserTimezone().

userStories.title: Fix getOvertradeMetrics Entry Time
userStories.description: As a trader, I want overtrade metrics grouped by entry time so that daily P&L is consistent across all analytics.
acceptanceCriteria: Change date grouping from exitTime to entryTime in getOvertradeMetrics procedure
Ensure userTimezone is fetched if not already available
userStories.notes: Fixed getOvertradingAnalysis (actual procedure name) to use trade.entryTime instead of trade.exitTime for date grouping. User timezone was already being fetched via getUserTimezone().

userStories.title: Fix getPsychometrics Entry Time
userStories.description: As a trader, I want psychometrics grouped by entry time so that daily analysis is consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: Change date grouping from exitTime to entryTime in getPsychometrics procedure
Ensure userTimezone is fetched if not already available
userStories.notes: Fixed getBehavioralPatterns procedure (actual name for 'getPsychometrics') to use trade.entryTime instead of trade.exitTime for date grouping in the Overtrading Tendency calculation. User timezone was already being fetched via getUserTimezone().

userStories.title: Audit All Analytics Date Grouping
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to audit all analytics procedures to ensure they use entry time and user timezone consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: Fix any procedures using exitTime for date grouping
userStories.notes: Audited all 6 specified procedures. Found getCalendarData, getPerformanceByDayOfWeek, getPerformanceByHour, getPerformanceBySession, getPerformanceByMonth all correctly use entryTime. getEquityCurve uses exitTime intentionally (equity curve plots cumulative P&L at trade close, not entry). Fixed getSymbolTrend which incorrectly used exitTime for month grouping - changed to entryTime.

userStories.title: Integration Tests for Timezone Date Grouping
userStories.description: As a developer, I want integration tests verifying trades are grouped by entry time in user's timezone.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests trade entered 11 PM EST closed next day appears on entry date
Tests getCalendarData returns correct dates for user timezone
Tests getOvertradeMetrics matches calendar data for same days
Tests daily journal trade grouping respects user timezone
userStories.notes: Created tests/integration/analytics/timezone-grouping.test.ts with comprehensive tests for: 1) Trade entry time grouping (11 PM EST case), 2) getCalendarData date accuracy, 3) getOvertradingAnalysis consistency with calendar data, 4) Daily journal trade grouping, 5) Pacific/Auckland timezone edge cases. Tests pass typecheck and build; full test run requires Docker.

userStories.title: Refactor CalendarHeatmap to Use Date Strings
userStories.description: As a trader, I want the analytics calendar heatmap to display trades on the correct day regardless of my browser's timezone.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace Date-based week generation with string-based approach
Use generateDateStringsInTimezone() for calendar grid generation
Works correctly when browser TZ differs from user TZ
Verify in browser with different timezone settings
userStories.notes: Refactored CalendarHeatmap to use string-based date handling. Replaced Date objects with generateDateStringsInTimezone() for calendar grid generation. Uses getDayOfWeekFromDateString() for week alignment, getMonthFromDateString() for month labels, and formatDateString() for tooltip display. All dates are now YYYY-MM-DD strings that match backend format directly.

userStories.title: Verify CalendarSidebar Timezone Consistency
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify the daily journal calendar sidebar uses the user's timezone consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: Verify date lookups use same format as backend data
Fix any inconsistencies found between frontend and backend date formats
Document if browser-local approach is intentional for journal dates
userStories.notes: Audited calendar-sidebar.tsx and found a bug: journal date lookups used toDateString() which converts to local time, causing date shift in browsers behind UTC. Fixed by adding getUTCDateString() utility for extracting dates from UTC midnight timestamps. Added documentation explaining the intentional browser-local calendar approach.

userStories.title: Integration Tests for Calendar Heatmap
userStories.description: As a developer, I want integration tests for calendar heatmap timezone edge cases.
acceptanceCriteria: Test date string generation for timezone Pacific/Auckland (+12)
Test date string generation for timezone America/Los_Angeles (-8)
Test week alignment starts on Sunday
Test month label positions are correct
userStories.notes: Created tests/integration/analytics/calendar-heatmap.test.ts with comprehensive tests for all timezone utility functions used by CalendarHeatmap: 1) generateDateStringsInTimezone for Pacific/Auckland (+12/+13) and America/Los_Angeles (-8), 2) getDayOfWeekFromDateString for week alignment (Sunday=0), 3) getMonthFromDateString for month labels, 4) formatDateString for tooltip display, 5) Year boundary handling (Dec 31/Jan 1), 6) Week alignment to Sunday for calendar grid, 7) Month transition detection, 8) Edge cases (leap years, DST). Tests pass typecheck and build.

userStories.title: Deprecate toDateString for Backend
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to document that toDateString() should not be used for backend date grouping.
acceptanceCriteria: Add JSDoc @deprecated to toDateString() in timezone.ts
Add comment explaining it's only for frontend calendar selection
Add migration guidance pointing to getDateStringInTimezone()
Search codebase for backend usages and fix or document them
userStories.notes: Added @deprecated JSDoc to toDateString() with clear migration guidance. Updated TIMEZONE_SYSTEM.md with 'FRONTEND ONLY' marker and added 'Mistake 4: Using toDateString on the Backend' section. Verified no backend usages exist in src/server/ (already clean).

userStories.title: Add Timezone Debug Info to Settings
userStories.description: As a trader, I want to see my current timezone configuration in settings so I can verify it's correct.
acceptanceCriteria: Show current timezone with UTC offset in settings page
Show current time in selected timezone (updates live)
Helps users verify their timezone is set correctly
Terminal design styling with monospace fonts
userStories.notes: Added 'Timezone Info' card to Trading tab in Settings. Shows timezone name with abbreviation, UTC offset, live updating current time (updates every second), and today's date. Uses Terminal design system: monospace fonts, uppercase tracking labels, primary accent color for time. Uses existing timezone utilities (getTimezoneAbbreviation, getTimezoneOffset, formatTimeInTimezone, formatDateInTimezone).
